mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of month-over-month RSAFS increase instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration that triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC is above -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signal a restrictive monetary policy response to inflation that jointly compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and lower real earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: The interaction term produces a positive rank correlation with 20-day forward returns, or the walk-forward Rank IC of the interaction falls below 0.03 in magnitude over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle economic expansions, a month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals supply-side contraction and impending earnings compression that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During late-cycle periods, month-over-month INDPRO declines coincide with positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances, or the walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.03 in magnitude.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level above 4.5% signals restrictive monetary policy that increases borrowing costs and equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 50% of instances, or the walk-forward Rank IC is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding labor income and aggregate demand that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 60-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of month-over-month PAYEMS growth instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signal broad-based consumer and investment demand strength that jointly drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: Simultaneous RSAFS and HOUST increases are followed by negative 20-day forward returns in more than 40% of joint-increase instances, or the interaction walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decrease in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) signals disinflationary pressure that reduces corporate input costs and supports margin expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 5-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of month-over-month CPIAUCSL decline instances.
